question_text: What causes objects to become electrically charged by friction?

options.label: A
options.text: Heat is created between the surfaces

options.label: B
options.text: Electrons are transferred from one material to another

options.label: C
options.text: The air between the objects becomes ionised

options.label: D
options.text: Protons move from one object to the other

explanation: When two materials rub together, electrons can be knocked off one material and transferred to another. This creates a charge imbalance: one object becomes positively charged (lost electrons) and the other becomes negatively charged (gained electrons).
